messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following pension advisory firm client privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"RetireWell collects your name, national insurance number, employment history, pension scheme details, and investment preferences to provide retirement planning advice. We process this under our FCA-regulated contract with you. All financial advice records are retained for 6 years per FCA rules. You have the right to access, correct, restrict, port, and object to your data. Contact compliance@retirewell.com. Our DPO is at dpo@retirewell.com. We use Salesforce Financial Services Cloud (UK instance) under a signed DPA. We notify the FCA and ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) for advisory services and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) for FCA regulatory requirements both stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Pension and employment history data can reveal disability, ill-health retirement, or financial vulnerability. No acknowledgement or safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Retirement planning advice and FCA regulatory compliance are clearly stated purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for retirement planning advisory services.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 6-year FCA-mandated retention is clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) listed. Erasure (Art. 17) absent — likely limited by FCA retention obligations, but this should be explained.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Salesforce Financial Services Cloud UK instance named under a signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] UK instance confirmed. Post-Brexit UK adequacy for EU clients should be referenced if applicable.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@retirewell.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour notification to FCA and ICO both stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (pension and employment data sensitivity not addressed), Art. 15-21 (erasure right absent without explanation)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that pension and employment history may reveal disability or ill-health data and state appropriate safeguards
- Add the erasure right with a clear explanation that it is limited by FCA record-keeping obligations under Art. 17(3)(b)
